title: lithomyrtus microphylla
intro: Lithomyrtus microphylla, also known as the 'Graciana' or 'Bottlebrush Myrtle', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Lithomyrtus microphylla include relieving fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Lithomyrtus microphylla is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture for its medicinal uses.
